formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
```json
[
  {
    "name": "Young's Modulus",
    "formula": "Y = F/A",
    "variables": "Y: Young's Modulus (N/m^2), F: Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Young's Modulus is a measure of a solid's stiffness"
  },
  {
    "name": "Bulk Modulus",
    "formula": "B = V/P",
    "variables": "B: Bulk Modulus (N/m^2), V: Volume (m^3), P: Pressure (N/m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Bulk Modulus is a measure of a solid's resistance to compression"
  },
  {
    "name": "Shear Modulus",
    "formula": "η = F/Aθ",
    "variables": "η: Shear Modulus (N/m^2), F: Shear Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2), θ: Shear Angle (radians)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Shear Modulus is a measure of a solid's resistance to shear stress"
  },
  {
    "name": "Poisson's Ratio",
    "formula": "σ = -l/l",
    "variables": "σ: Poisson's Ratio (unitless), l: Original Length (m), l': New Length (m)",
    "units": "unitless",
    "notes": "Poisson's Ratio is a measure of a solid's lateral strain response to a longitudinal tensile loading"
  },
  {
    "name": "Lateral Strain",
    "formula": "lateral strain = Δl/l",
    "variables": "lateral strain: Lateral Strain (unitless), Δl: Change in Length (m), l: Original Length (m)",
    "units": "unitless",
    "notes": "Lateral Strain is the change in length perpendicular to the direction of the applied force"
  },
  {
    "name": "Longitudinal Strain",
    "formula": "ε = Δl/l",
    "variables": "ε: Longitudinal Strain (unitless), Δl: Change in Length (m), l: Original Length (m)",
    "units": "unitless",
    "notes": "Longitudinal Strain is the change in length along the direction of the applied force"
  },
  {
    "name": "Hooke's Law",
    "formula": "F = kx",
    "variables": "F: Force (N), k: Spring Constant (N/m), x: Displacement (m)",
    "units": "N",
    "notes": "Hooke's Law describes the relationship between force and displacement in a spring"
  },
  {
    "name": "Stress",
    "formula": "σ = F/A",
    "variables": "σ: Stress (N/m^2), F: Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Stress is the force per unit area"
  },
  {
    "name": "Strain",
    "formula": "ε = Δl/l",
    "variables": "ε: Strain (unitless), Δl: Change in Length (m), l: Original Length (m)",
    "units": "unitless",
    "notes": "Strain is the fractional change in length"
  },
  {
    "name": "Bulk Stress",
    "formula": "P = F/A",
    "variables": "P: Bulk Stress (N/m^2), F: Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Bulk Stress is the pressure applied to a material"
  },
  {
    "name": "Shear Stress",
    "formula": "τ = F/A",
    "variables": "τ: Shear Stress (N/m^2), F: Shear Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Shear Stress is the force per unit area applied parallel to the surface"
  },
  {
    "name": "Tensile Stress",
    "formula": "σ = F/A",
    "variables": "σ: Tensile Stress (N/m^2), F: Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Tensile Stress is the force per unit area applied in the direction of the material"
  },
  {
    "name": "Compressive Stress",
    "formula": "σ = F/A",
    "variables": "σ: Compressive Stress (N/m^2), F: Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Compressive Stress is the force per unit area applied opposite to the direction of the material"
  },
  {
    "name": "Tensile Strength",
    "formula": "σ = F/A",
    "variables": "σ: Tensile Strength (N/m^2), F: Force (N), A: Cross-sectional Area (m^2)",
    "units": "N/m^2",
    "notes": "Tensile Strength is the maximum stress a material can withstand before failing"
